LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT KATHLEEN BARISH MOTION TO STRIKE UNDER THE CALIFORNIA ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE; CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 425.16. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED. LATE OPPOSITION CONSIDERED. "REPLY TO REPLY" AND "SUPPLEMENT TO OPPOSITION" STRICKEN AS UNTIMELY. THE LAWSUIT ARISES OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY. (COMPLAINT PARAGRAPHS 2 AND 5.) CCP SECTION 425.16 IS BROADLY INTERPRETED. CIVIL CODE SECTION 47 PRIVILEGE APPLIES TO THE ALLEGED STATEMENTS THAT HAVE SOME RELATION TO A JUDICIAL PROCEEDING. THE BURDEN SHIFTS TO PLAINTIFF WHO OFFERS NO LEGALLY ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATING A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Ntc And Motion For Award Of Atty'S Fees; Memo Of P And A; Declaration(S) Of Thomas R Burke, Karl Olson,Steven Abern
Off Calendar Mar-01-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT KATHLEEN BARISH Motion for Award of Attorney's Fees. Appearances: Phil Walker, Esq. of Phil Walker Law (415) 816-3527 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Steven Sheriff Abern, Esq. of Haapala, Thompson & Abern, LLP (510) 763-2324 appearing for Defendant and moving party; Karl Olson, Esq. of Ram & Olson LLP (415) 433-4949 appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for attorney fees granted, defendant awarded $42,928.20 attorney fees and costs. Hourly rates reasonable. Work performed reasonable and necessary, and counsels' declarations support the award which is based on hours expended times hourly rates. Order signed in open Court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-16-2010 Continued To Apr-23-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Under The California Anti-Slapp Statute; Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16; Declarations Of Karl Olson And Kathleen M. Barish
Off Calendar Jan-07-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT KATHLEEN BARISH Motion to Strike under The California Anti-Slapp Statute; Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16. Appearances: Steven Sheriff Abern, Esq. of Haapala, Altura, Thompson & Abern LLP (510) 763-2324, appearing for Defendant and moving party; Karl Olson, Esq. of Ram & Olson LLP (415) 433-4949 also, appearing for Defendant and Moving party. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted. Late opposition considered. "Reply to Reply" and "Supplement to Opposition" stricken as untimely. The lawsuit arises out of protected activity. (Complaint paragraphs 2 and 5.) CCP section 425.16 is broadly interpreted. Civil Code section 47 privilege applies to the alleged statements that have some relation to a judicial proceeding. The burden shifts to plaintiff who offers no legally admissible evidence demonstrating a probability of prevailing on the merits. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
